---
title: "Corporate Entity Resolution Engine (CORE)"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

## Transparent. Reviewable. Reproducible. Cumulative.

> **Entity resolution does not merely produce analytical datasets.**
>
> **It produces scientific knowledge.**

<p align="center">
  <img src="assets/core_hero_figure.svg"
       alt="CORE Framework Overview"
       width="700">
</p>

<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 20px; margin-bottom: 50px;">

<p style="font-size: 1.15rem; margin-bottom: 20px;">
Try CORE directly in your browser.
</p>

<a href="https://corematch.shinyapps.io/core/"
   target="_blank"
   rel="noopener noreferrer"
   style="
      display: inline-block;
      background-color: #2f4159;
      color: #ffffff;
      text-decoration: none;
      padding: 16px 36px;
      border-radius: 10px;
      font-size: 1.2rem;
      font-weight: 600;
      line-height: 1.2;
   ">
Launch Live Demonstrator
</a>

</div>

CORE is an open-source research software framework designed to support transparent, reviewable, reproducible, and cumulative entity resolution in empirical research.

Rather than treating entity resolution as a disposable data-cleaning task, CORE approaches entity resolution as a scientific process in which computational methods, expert judgement, and validated knowledge work together to produce trustworthy analytical datasets.

Originally developed for corporate entity resolution, CORE has been intentionally designed as a general methodology capable of supporting transparent entity resolution across a broad range of empirical research domains.

---

## Why CORE?

Traditional entity resolution workflows often end once a matched dataset has been produced.

Intermediate decisions, reviewer knowledge, and methodological reasoning frequently disappear with the project, forcing future researchers to reconstruct work that has already been completed.

CORE adopts a different philosophy.

> **Validated entity resolution decisions are scientific knowledge and should be preserved rather than discarded at the conclusion of individual research projects.**

The framework therefore combines:

- transparent computational workflows;
- explicit expert review through the Review Queue;
- preservation of validated scientific judgement;
- reusable knowledge that supports future empirical research.

---

## Core Principles

CORE has been designed around five methodological principles:

- **Transparency**
- **Scientific Judgement**
- **Reproducibility**
- **Knowledge Preservation**
- **Methodological Continuity**

Together, these principles establish entity resolution as a documented and reproducible scientific activity rather than a project-specific preprocessing task.

---

## Framework Components

The CORE ecosystem currently consists of six major components:

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Entity Resolution Engine | Computational entity resolution workflow |
| Review Queue | Transparent expert evaluation of uncertain matches |
| Learning Layer | Preservation of validated review history |
| Dictionary Infrastructure | Reusable project and community knowledge |
| Documentation Portal | Methodological and architectural documentation |
| Research Software Infrastructure | Reproducibility and software quality support |

---

## Methodological Workflow

CORE implements a six-stage research workflow.

```text
Raw Data
    ↓
Data Acquisition
    ↓
Data Preparation
    ↓
Entity Resolution
    ↓
Expert Validation
    ↓
Knowledge Preservation
    ↓
Analysis-Ready Outputs
```

Unlike conventional matching pipelines, CORE deliberately preserves uncertainty whenever computational evidence alone cannot support reliable scientific conclusions.

---

## Learning Philosophy

The CORE Learning Layer preserves validated review decisions without automating judgement.

It does not:

- rescore candidates;
- modify confidence scores;
- automatically accept matches;
- automatically reject matches;
- override expert judgement.

Its role is deliberately simple:

```text
Preserve review history.
Do not automate judgement.
```

Examples of review history shown to users include:

```text
Accepted once before.
Accepted twice before.
Rejected once before.
No previous review history.
```

This design protects transparency while allowing validated review knowledge to remain available for future research.

---

## Current Status

CORE v1.0.0 currently includes:

- a stable corporate entity resolution engine;
- a transparent Review Queue;
- validated review-history preservation;
- dictionary management infrastructure;
- a public documentation website;
- an interactive Shiny demonstrator;
- integration and testthat test suites;
- an automated project code audit;
- open-source repository infrastructure.

---

## Explore CORE

::: {.callout-tip}
## Try CORE Online

The public demonstrator allows users to explore the Review Queue, inspect match diagnostics, record expert decisions, and view previous review history without installing the software.

[Launch the CORE Demonstrator](https://corematch.shinyapps.io/core/){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Source Code

The complete source code, release history, citation metadata, and contribution guidelines are available in the GitHub repository.

[View CORE on GitHub](https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE){target="_blank"}
:::

---

## Feedback and Suggestions

::: {.callout-important}
## Help Improve CORE

CORE is currently in its first public release. Feedback from researchers, data scientists, research software developers, and potential users is highly appreciated.

Please use GitHub Issues to share:

- bug reports;
- usability suggestions;
- documentation improvements;
- feature requests;
- methodological questions;
- potential research applications.

[Provide Feedback on GitHub](https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE/issues/new/choose){target="_blank"}
:::

All public feedback remains visible and traceable through the project's GitHub Issues page.

[View Existing Issues](https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE/issues){target="_blank"}

---

## Documentation

The documentation website provides complementary material for understanding and using CORE.

| Page | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Installation | Install CORE and launch the demonstrator |
| Philosophy | Scientific motivation and conceptual foundations |
| Architecture | Software architecture and design principles |
| Workflow | Information flow across the framework |
| Methodology | Methodological foundations of CORE |
| Tutorials | Practical examples and user workflows |
| Demonstrator | Information about the interactive application |
| News | Release history and project updates |

---

## Long-Term Vision

The long-term objective of CORE extends beyond individual entity resolution algorithms.

The project aims to become a complete open research software platform supporting transparent, reviewable, reproducible, and cumulative entity resolution across multiple empirical research domains.

Future development priorities include:

- additional tutorials and worked examples;
- community-maintained dictionaries;
- large-scale benchmarking;
- collaborative review workflows;
- cross-domain entity resolution;
- continued improvements to documentation and usability.

These developments will preserve the central CORE principle that expert judgement should remain transparent, documented, and reproducible.

---

## Citation

If CORE contributes to your research, please cite the software using the citation metadata provided in the repository.

Citation information is available through:

- `CITATION.cff`;
- the GitHub release page;
- future archived releases and DOI registration.

---

## Public Links

- **Documentation:** <https://yrtsv.github.io/CORE/>
- **Live Demonstrator:** <https://corematch.shinyapps.io/core/>
- **Source Code:** <https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE>
- **Feedback and Issues:** <https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE/issues>
- **Latest Release:** <https://github.com/yrtsv/CORE/releases/latest>
